refinement_sections.content: This report will analyze CPU scheduling performance for a new multi-core processor system. We need to find out which scheduling algorithm works best for the workloads at Tech Innovators Corp.

We will compare at least three scheduling algorithms. Some common ones are First-Come, First-Served (FCFS), Shortest Job Next (SJN), and Round Robin. I will choose these because they are often used and have different approaches.

For the workloads, we need to pick some that represent real-world usage. I think we could use workloads like web server requests, batch processing jobs, and interactive applications. These cover different types of tasks that the system might handle.

We will look at response time, throughput, and fairness. Response time is how quickly a task gets started, throughput is how many tasks are completed in a certain time, and fairness is about how equally tasks are treated.
I will run experiments using each scheduling algorithm with the selected workloads. I will measure the response time, throughput, and fairness for each algorithm and then compare the results.

I assume that the workloads can be simulated accurately and that the multi-core processor can handle multiple tasks at once without issues. Also, I assume that the test environment is stable and does not change during the experiments.
After gathering the data, I will summarize the findings and recommend the best scheduling algorithm based on the analysis.
